case_brief: Thomas Bradley, a 47-year-old logistics coordinator at Global Freight Solutions, was terminated after a workplace investigation confirmed he had made repeated sexually inappropriate comments to three female colleagues over a 14-month period. The investigation was triggered by a formal HR complaint. Two of the three complainants provided written statements; a third provided verbal testimony corroborated by a fourth witness who overheard one incident. Bradley received a written warning after the first complaint 10 months prior, which he signed acknowledging the behavior policy. Bradley claims the termination was actually motivated by his having filed an unpaid overtime wage claim with the Department of Labor three months before termination. However, the company's investigation was initiated by HR, not management, and was prompted by a new complaint unrelated to the DOL claim. The timing of the DOL claim and termination, while close, is explained by the independent investigation timeline. Employment at-will in Florida (11th Circuit). Progressive discipline policy was followed.
